introSection:
Formula Rossa (Arabic: فورمولا روسّا) is a launched roller coaster located at Ferrari World Abu Dhabi in Abu Dhabi, Abu Dhabi, United Arab Emirates. Designed by Intamin, Formula Rossa was the fastest roller coaster in the world at opening, reaching speeds of. This record has since been surpassed by Falcon's Flight at Six Flags Qiddiya. Riders must wear protective glasses due to the high speeds and occurrence of sandstorms in Abu Dhabi.

The ride was closed from 22 January 2024 to 22 December 2024. Formula Rossa reopened on 23 December 2024.

sections.title: History
sections.content: Formula Rossa was planned to open with the park on 27 October 2010. However, the opening of the park was postponed. It officially opened on 4 November. It was the last Accelerator Coaster installation from Intamin as they discontinued the model.

sections.title: Trains
sections.content: 4 trains with 4 cars per train. Riders are arranged 2 across in 2 rows for a total of 16 riders per train.

sections.title: Theme
sections.content: The roller coaster was marketed as emulating "the thrilling sensation of being in a Ferrari F1 car", during construction of the ride.

sections.title: In video games
sections.content: A very similar roller coaster, Sprint 500, is available as a ride choice on Frontier's Planet Coaster simulation game.

fullText:
Formula Rossa (Arabic: فورمولا روسّا) is a launched roller coaster located at Ferrari World Abu Dhabi in Abu Dhabi, Abu Dhabi, United Arab Emirates. Designed by Intamin, Formula Rossa was the fastest roller coaster in the world at opening, reaching speeds of. This record has since been surpassed by Falcon's Flight at Six Flags Qiddiya. Riders must wear protective glasses due to the high speeds and occurrence of sandstorms in Abu Dhabi.

The ride was closed from 22 January 2024 to 22 December 2024. Formula Rossa reopened on 23 December 2024.

Formula Rossa was planned to open with the park on 27 October 2010. However, the opening of the park was postponed. It officially opened on 4 November. It was the last Accelerator Coaster installation from Intamin as they discontinued the model.



4 trains with 4 cars per train. Riders are arranged 2 across in 2 rows for a total of 16 riders per train.

The roller coaster was marketed as emulating "the thrilling sensation of being in a Ferrari F1 car", during construction of the ride.

A very similar roller coaster, Sprint 500, is available as a ride choice on Frontier's Planet Coaster simulation game.
